chapter_title.ar: باب كَرَاهِيَةِ أَنْ يَبِيتَ الْإِنْسَانُ وَحْدَهُ وَ الْخِصَالِ الْمَنْهِيِّ عَنْهَا لِعِلَّةٍ مَخُوفَةٍ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 69 – Abhorrence for the human being to sleep alone, and the characteristics Prohibited about it for the reason of fearfulness

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/448)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/448)
text: قَالَ إِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ أَشَدَّ مَا يَهُمُّ بِالْإِنْسَانِ حِينَ يَكُونُ وَحْدَهُ خَالِياً لَا أَرَى أَنْ يَرْقُدَ وَحْدَهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Aban Al Ahmar, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The Satan<sup>la</sup> is the most troublesome with the human being when he happens to be alone, isolated. I<sup>asws</sup> should not see him sleeping alone’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from ‘Abd Allah ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Aban al-Ahmar from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that Satan is the strongest against man when he is alone without anything and I say that he must not sleep alone.’”
